developer.comment: Y Combinator-backed Glass Devtools, Inc.; project published under the voideditor GitHub org.

first-release.comment: First public beta release (with autocomplete) Jan 19, 2025 per the changelog. The website/project 'Early Launch' entry is dated Oct 1, 2024; using the first usable editor beta as the public release.

status.comment: Checked 2026-06-05: GitHub repo archived (read-only) on Jun 2, 2026; README states Void is deprecated and no longer accepting contributions. Builds remain downloadable and functional, so it stays in scope, but lifecycle status is discontinued.

license.comment: Apache-2.0 licensed (VS Code fork). OSI-approved open source.

specialization.comment: Broad coding editor; not positioned for a specific niche.

interface.comment: Standalone AI-native editor built as a fork of VS Code.

host-ides.comment: Forked VS Code editor that runs as its own standalone application.

open-source.comment: Agent/editor source is Apache-2.0 on GitHub.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Full standalone editor (VS Code fork), not an extension.

default-model-provider.comment: No single default vendor model; Void is BYO-LLM and connects directly to whichever provider/model the user configures (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, local, etc.).

model-choice.comment: Users choose among many models/providers from within the editor.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Core feature: keys stored locally and used to call providers directly with no middle-layer backend.

local-model-support.comment: Supports local/self-hosted models via Ollama and vLLM (DeepSeek, Llama, Qwen, Gemma, Mistral, etc.).

multi-file-editing.comment: Agent mode can create and edit files across the codebase; multi-file background editing with file syncing.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Agent mode chains steps — searches the codebase, reads/writes files, and runs terminal commands toward a task.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Agent mode can control the terminal as part of its loop.

mcp-support.comment: Native MCP support added in the June 5, 2025 update (v1.4.x).

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: No documented support for spawning subagents or parallel agent workers; Void runs a single agent loop. Marked null rather than false as the docs do not explicitly address it.

codebase-indexing.comment: Agent mode searches the codebase, but no documentation confirms a built semantic/embeddings index vs. on-demand file reads. Indeterminate.

planning-mode.comment: Void offers Agent / Gather (read-only) / Chat modes; Gather is read-only exploration but not documented as an explicit plan-then-execute phase. Indeterminate.

multimodal-input.comment: No clear documentation of image/screenshot input. Indeterminate; depends partly on the underlying model selected.

browser-computer-use.comment: No built-in browser/GUI driving capability documented; agent acts on files and terminal only.

operating-mode.comment: Ctrl+L chat, Ctrl+K inline edits, Tab autocomplete, and an Agent mode that edits/runs with the user in the loop.

inline-completion.comment: Tab autocomplete (FIM-model based), introduced at the initial beta.

background-async-execution.comment: Local desktop editor; the agent runs in the active session rather than dispatching detached/cloud background tasks.

persistent-memory.comment: No documentation of cross-session project memory or learned preferences. Indeterminate.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Checkpoints feature lets users jump between / revert LLM edits (added Apr 14, 2025).

git-integration.comment: Inherits VS Code's native git integration (staging, diffs, commits).

pr-creation.comment: No documented native PR-creation feature beyond inherited VS Code git/extension capabilities. Indeterminate.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: No clearly documented project-level instruction-file convention (rules / AGENTS.md style) for Void's agent. Indeterminate.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via MCP servers (external tools) and inherits the VS Code extension ecosystem.

permission-model.comment: Agent mode acts on files/terminal with user review; a read-only Gather mode restricts the agent to read operations, giving configurable autonomy.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Runs entirely on the user's machine; can point at fully self-hosted/local models with no vendor backend involved.

privacy-mode.comment: Void sends no data through a private backend — keys stored locally and requests go directly to the chosen provider, so the vendor never receives or trains on user code.

enterprise-controls.comment: Open-source local editor from a small team; no SSO/RBAC/audit/VPC enterprise governance offered.

pricing-model.comment: The editor itself is free and open source; the user pays only their own LLM provider via BYO-key. No Void subscription.

free-tier.comment: Editor is entirely free to download and use; only model API costs (if using a paid provider) apply.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: No paid plan for the editor; free and open source (BYO model key). 0 per the guideline for a genuine free tier covering core use.

maturity.comment: Reached only beta (latest changelog v1.4.x, mid-2025) and development is now paused/archived as of June 2026; usable but never reached polished GA.

community-size.comment: ~28.8k GitHub stars (notable interest) but a single-vendor project that is now deprecated/archived, so the active user community is small.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: As a VS Code fork it can use VS Code extensions, and supports MCP servers as third-party tooling.

project-memory-files.comment: No documented auto-loaded project instruction-file convention specific to Void's agent. Marked none for the always-on memory surface.

memory-scopes.comment: No documented memory/rules scoping levels. Indeterminate.

auto-memory.comment: No automatic cross-session memory the agent writes on its own is documented.

skill-support.comment: No packaged reusable skill/recipe mechanism beyond MCP tools and built-in modes; remaining Skills attributes left empty/null.

sandboxing-technology.comment: Local VS Code fork running on the user's machine; agent acts on files and terminal with the user's full privileges. No OS sandbox, container, VM, or filesystem jail documented; safety relies on per-action approval prompts.

permission-granularity.comment: Checked 2026-06-12: Agent mode prompts for approval on each terminal command individually (per-command). Gather mode is a read-only variant of Agent mode (read/search but no modify/edit), giving a read-vs-write distinction at the mode level. No command-prefix allowlist: the user-defined auto-approve-rules request (Issue #629) was never implemented and the repo is archived.

rule-precedence.comment: No declarative allow/deny rule engine. The pattern-based auto-approve proposal (Issue #629) remained an open, unimplemented feature request; every command prompts individually.

permission-configurability.comment: User chooses among preset modes — Agent (full), Gather (read-only), and normal Chat — but cannot author declarative allow/deny rules or programmable permission hooks. The proposed user-defined rule engine (Issue #629) was never shipped.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No documented default deny patterns for secrets (.env, key files); Agent mode can read/write files in the workspace and no built-in secret gating ships. Protection would rely on per-action approval by the user.

persistent-allowlist.comment: No saved/remembered approvals. The feature to define persistent auto-approve command rules (Issue #629) was requested but never implemented; the agent re-prompts for each command.

unsupervised-mode.comment: No documented full-autonomy / auto-approve / YOLO bypass. Agent mode requires manual approval for each terminal command; the auto-approve request (Issue #629) was never merged before the project was archived.

network-access-control.comment: No network gating during agent runs is documented; as a local editor with full user privileges, agent-run commands have unrestricted outbound access. (LLM calls go direct to the user-configured provider via BYO key.)

default-permission-posture.comment: Checked 2026-06-12: out of the box, Agent mode asks the user to approve each terminal command it wants to run (no shipped auto-approve, per Issue #629). File edits are surfaced for review with checkpoint/revert support. Closest unconfigured posture is prompt-each-action.
